hero.subtitle: Your circle, your data, nobody else's. An open-source alternative to Life360 where the server never sees your coordinates.
hero.noAds: No ads. No trackers. No data sales.

e2e.title: End-to-end encrypted
e2e.body: Locations, place names and geofences are sealed on your device. The server stores ciphertext blobs it cannot read — only your circle holds the key.

noSelling.title: No ads, no trackers, no data sales
noSelling.body: There is no ad SDK, no third-party analytics, nothing to sell. Privacy is the product, not the cost of admission.

selfHost.title: Open source & self-hostable
selfHost.body: The whole stack is open source (AGPL) — audit it, or run it on your own server if you'd rather. Either way the trust model is the same; most people just use our cloud.

antiStalk.title: Anti-stalking by design
antiStalk.body: No hidden mode. A reporter always shows a visible notification, one tap reveals exactly who can see you, and leaving is instant and unilateral.

create.title: Create a circle
create.body: Start a private circle for your family or household. A fresh encryption key is generated on your device.

invite.title: Invite family by link
invite.body: Share a one-tap invite link. The circle key rides in the link fragment and never reaches the server.

seeMap.title: See everyone on a live map
seeMap.body: Watch your circle move in real time on an encrypted live map — decrypted only in your app, never on the server.

oss.title: Open source, top to bottom
oss.body: The server is AGPL-3.0 and the clients are MIT. Audit the crypto, run it yourself, or send a pull request. Nothing about your privacy is hidden behind a closed binary.

faq.title: Frequently asked
faq.a1: Yes. Location pings, place names and geofences are encrypted on your device with a circle key that never leaves your circle. The server only ever receives and stores ciphertext it cannot decrypt. The code is open — read the threat model and check for yourself.
faq.q2: Is it free and open source?
faq.a2: Yes. The server is licensed AGPL-3.0 and the clients (web and app) are MIT. There is no paid tier gating your privacy — self-host for free, or use our cloud.
faq.a3: Yes — the whole backend is open source (one Go binary that also serves the web app and the APK), so you can run it on your own server. It is a technical path (clone the repo, set your secrets, bring the stack up with Docker), not a one-click product — most people just use our cloud.
faq.a4: The Android app is distributed off-Play so the build you install is exactly the source you can audit. Each release publishes its SHA-256, and the in-app updater verifies that digest before installing — no store intermediary, no silent repackaging.
faq.q5: Do you have an iOS app?
faq.a5: Android first, iOS later. The app is being built Android-first; an iOS client is on the roadmap. In the meantime the web app works in any modern browser.

landing.finalCta: Keep your circle close — and private.

download.title: Download Aul
download.subtitle: Private family location, end-to-end encrypted. Get the Android app below, or open the web app to try it in your browser.

android.title: Android
android.noApk: This build has no APK attached yet — build from source.
android.installBody: Aul is distributed outside the Play Store, so your source stays auditable. When you open the APK, Android will ask you to enable “install unknown apps” for your browser — allow it once. After the first install the app updates itself: every update is downloaded and its SHA-256 verified before it installs.

notPublished.body: Coming soon. No signed build has been published yet. In the meantime you can build the app from source.

error.title: Couldn't load the latest release
error.body: Something went wrong reaching the server. Check your connection and try again.

ios.title: iOS
ios.subtitle: Coming soon — App Store.
ios.body: Aul is being built Android-first; an iPhone app is on the roadmap. Until then, the web app works in any modern browser.

download.footer: Every release is reproducible from source and its SHA-256 is published above.

auth.tagline: Private family location, end-to-end encrypted. We don't see your data — check the code.
auth.disclaimer: The web dashboard is a convenience with a weaker trust model than the app (its code is served by the server). Use the app for anything sensitive.

join.missingKey: This invite link is missing its circle key.
join.failed: Could not join with this link — it may be expired.

empty.title: Start your circle
empty.body: Create a family circle, then invite the people you love.

prompt.sos: Send an SOS to your circle. Add a short message (optional):

dashboard.noKey: This circle's key isn't on this device, so locations can't be decrypted here. Open your invite link in this browser to add it.

connection.stale: Locations may be stale — last connected {{ago}}

who.title: Who can see me
who.body: Your encrypted location is shared with {{circleName}}. Only its members can decrypt it — the server stores ciphertext it cannot read.

members.heading: People
members.accuracyTitle: How vague this fix is: the device is somewhere within this radius, not exactly on the pin.
members.wifiHint: This PC has no GPS — it locates itself from nearby Wi-Fi, and it won't get sharper. Use your phone for a precise pin.
members.staleTitle: This location may be out of date — this device hasn't reported in a while, so it may have moved.
members.centerOnMap: Tap to centre the map on them
members.empty: Just you so far. Invite your family below.
members.mute: Mute {{name}} — stop their notifications reaching you
members.unmute: Unmute {{name}} — let their notifications reach you again
members.removeConfirm: Remove {{name}} from this circle? They stop seeing everyone, and stop sharing with you.
members.rotateAfterRemove: Removed. They still hold the old circle key, so they could read data sent from now on. Rotate the circle key now?

places.heading: Places
places.centreSet: Centre set. Adjust the radius, then save.
places.clickMap: Click the map to set the centre.
places.empty: No places yet. Add Home, Work, School…

sos.title: SOS · someone needs help
sos.encrypted: Encrypted — add this circle's key to read the details

geofence.nobody: Nobody inside a place right now.

verify.title: Verify devices
verify.description: Compare these emoji with the other person, in person or on a call. If they match, no one has tampered with your keys.
verify.noIdentity: No identity key on this device.
verify.noDevicesWhy: A safety code always compares two devices — yours and someone else's. You're the only device in this circle with a key, so there's no pair to make a code from yet. Invite someone (or add a second device of your own), and a code will appear here to compare.

invite.title: Invite to your circle
invite.note: This link contains your circle key in the part after #, which the server never receives. Share it only with family.

share.title: Live share link
share.intro: Creates a link that shows your live location to one person — no account needed. Whoever opens it first keeps it; nobody else can. It stops at the deadline, or the moment you revoke it.
share.error: Could not create a share link
share.note: The key that decrypts your position is the part after #, which the server never receives. Anyone holding the whole link can watch you until it ends.
share.claimed: Open — a viewer has this link
share.unclaimed: Not opened yet — the first device to open it keeps it
share.noKeyHere: Made in another browser: its link can't be shown here, but you can still revoke it.
share.revokeConfirm: Revoke this link? Whoever is watching stops seeing you immediately.

view.title: Live location

waiting.title: Waiting for the first position
waiting.body: The link works. There's nothing to show until their device sends its first update.

expired.title: This link has expired
expired.body: The live share is over. Ask for a new link if you still need one.

forbidden.title: This link is open somewhere else
forbidden.body: A live-share link works on one device only, and another one opened this first. Ask for a new link.

notFound.title: This link doesn't exist
notFound.body: It may have been mistyped or already cleaned up. Ask for a new link.

missingKey.title: This link is missing its key
missingKey.body: The part after # is what decrypts the location. Copy the whole link and open it again.

badKey.title: This link's key doesn't fit
badKey.body: The part after # can't decrypt this location. Copy the whole link again, exactly as you got it.

offline.title: Can't reach the server
offline.body: Check your connection — this page keeps trying.

view.privacy: You see only this person, only until the countdown ends. The server stores the position encrypted and cannot read it.
view.focusNote: The map hides when this tab loses focus.

prefs.title: Preferences
prefs.serverDisabled: These optional features have been turned off by the server operator, so they can't be enabled here.
prefs.intro: Optional, on-device features. Everything below is computed from data already decrypted in your browser — nothing new is sent to the server. All off until you turn them on.

arrival.title: Arrival alerts & ETA
arrival.desc: Rough ETAs in the places feed, plus a notification when someone arrives.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.granted: Allowed — arrival alerts can pop up while this tab is open.

push.title: Background notifications
push.desc: Alerts even when this tab is closed. Your browser is woken by a sealed blob the server relays but cannot read — it never learns who arrived where.
push.unsupported: This browser doesn't support background notifications.
push.denied: Notifications are blocked in your browser settings.
push.failed: Couldn't turn background notifications on. Try again later.
push.serverOff: This server has no push keys configured, so alerts only appear while this tab is open.

prefs.backgroundNote: With background notifications off, alerts only appear while this tab is open.

circlesDash.title: My circles
circlesDash.subtitle: Every circle you're in, and what you give each one.
circlesDash.empty: You're not in any circle yet.

visibility.title: Share my location
visibility.on: This circle sees where you are.
visibility.off: Paused — your location isn't shared with this circle.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.on: This circle's members can send you notifications.
notifications.off: Muted — notifications from this circle's members don't reach you.

profile.title: Your profile in this circle
profile.nicknamePlaceholder: How you appear to this circle
profile.tooLarge: That image is too large. Try a smaller crop.
profile.imageError: Couldn't read that image. Try another.
profile.saveError: Couldn't save your profile. Try again.

circles.leaveConfirm: Leave this circle? You'll stop sharing your location with it and stop seeing its members.
circles.soleOwner: You're the only owner, so you can't just leave. Delete this circle for everyone instead?
circles.deleteConfirm: Delete "{{name}}" for everyone? This permanently removes the circle and all its data. This cannot be undone.

errors.rate_limited: Too many attempts. Please wait a moment and try again.
errors.account_locked: Too many attempts — this account is temporarily locked. Try again later.
errors.internal_error: Something went wrong on our end. Please try again.
errors.timeout: The request timed out. Please try again.
errors.forbidden: You don’t have permission to do that.
errors.network: Can’t reach the server. Check your connection and try again.

start.title: Start your circle
start.body: Pick how you want to begin.

create.title: Create a circle
create.body: Start a new circle on Aul, then invite your people.

join.title: Join an existing circle
join.body: Paste an invite link you were sent.
join.error: That doesn't look like a valid invite link.
